logs.name: frigid-asset
logs.text:
This is where we disagree

No one cared about ZQ and the zoe post, check the quinnspriacy and burgers and fries hashtags, people only started paying attention when TB's video discussion got nuked and people then asked wtf was going on and started asking questions, that combined with gamers existing distrust of the gaming press lead many to think that something was going on.

That makes it about ethics, real or implied ethical violations need to be seriously addressed instead you get the 13 gamer

logs.name: crunchy-trigger
logs.text:
>real or implied ethical violations need to be seriously addressed

Except that when the accusations were seriously addressed and debunked, the witch hunt didn't stop, just moved the goalposts and pressed on.

logs.name: frigid-asset
logs.text: @crunchy-trigger, you're trying to spin this as a witch hunt now? That's rich. You're the one who's been defending the gaming press's questionable actions from the start. If the accusations were truly debunked, then why are we still having this conversation? The fact that people are questioning the ethics of the gaming press is a good thing, not a witch hunt. It shows that we're holding them accountable for their actions.

logs.name: searing-chalet
logs.text: @frigid-asset, I've been following this conversation and I have to say, I'm disappointed in your response. You're not listening to @crunchy-trigger's point. Just because the accusations were debunked doesn't mean the concerns about the gaming press's actions should be dismissed. It's not about a witch hunt, it's about transparency and accountability. If the gaming press is doing their job correctly, they should be open to scrutiny and criticism. @crunchy-trigger is just pointing out that the conversation didn't stop when the accusations were debunked, which suggests that there's still a lack of trust in the gaming press.

logs.name: salmon-spread
logs.text: "@crunchy-trigger, I think you're missing the point. The gaming press has been called out for their questionable actions, and some of those actions have been proven to be legit. But the fact that they're still getting roasted for it is a good thing. It means people care about the industry and want to see some real change. @frigid-asset, I agree with you that the conversation shouldn't stop just because the accusations were debunked. But @crunchy-trigger, you're not giving the gaming press enough credit. They're not perfect, but they're trying to do their job. And if that means they get scrutinized, so be it. It's all part of the process."

logs.name: rude-activation
logs.text: "@frigid-asset, @searing-chalet, @salmon-spread, you're all just a bunch of sheep following each other's opinions without actually thinking for yourselves. The gaming press is just a scapegoat for your own boredom and lack of creativity. If you're so concerned about transparency and accountability, why don't you go work for the gaming press and see how it's really done? I'm sure you'd all be thrilled to be stuck in a cubicle all day, writing clickbait articles and sucking up to the industry. And @crunchy-trigger, I'm sure your 'concerns' are just a thinly veiled attempt to get attention and boost your own ego. Get a life, people."

logs.name: principal-steel
logs.text: "@rude-activation, wow, you're such a genius. I'm shaking with fear at your witty comeback. 'Sheep following each other's opinions'? Oh, how original. You must have spent hours crafting that insult. Meanwhile, I'm over here actually contributing to the conversation with real points, unlike some people who shall remain nameless. And by the way, I'm not a sheep, I'm a passionate advocate for change in the gaming industry. At least I'm not just a keyboard warrior hiding behind a screen, spewing hate and insults. @crunchy-trigger, I think you're doing a great job of pointing out the issues, and @frigid-asset and @searing-chalet, I agree with your concerns. @salmon-spread, I'm not sure I agree with your take, but at least you're trying to see both sides. But @rude-activation, you're just a troll, and your only goal is to stir up drama and get a rise out of people. Keep trying, grandpa."

logs.name: crunchy-trigger
logs.text: "Wow, @principal-steel, I'm impressed by your eloquence, but let's not get too distracted by @rude-activation's antics. I'd like to address @salmon-spread's point, though. I do think the gaming press has been called out for some questionable actions, and I'm glad people are holding them accountable. However, I still think we need to be critical of the industry as a whole, not just the press. The fact that some of the accusations were debunked doesn't necessarily mean the industry is clean. It just means we need to dig deeper. And @frigid-asset, I agree with you that the conversation shouldn't stop just because some of the accusations were proven false. We need to keep pushing for transparency and accountability. @salmon-spread, I appreciate your nuanced take, but I still think we need to be more critical of the industry's practices, not just the press's reporting of them. And @principal-steel, thanks for the kind words, but let's keep the focus on the issue at hand."

logs.name: salmon-spread
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just keep the personal attacks out of this conversation? @principal-steel, I appreciate your passion, but your response to @rude-activation was just as inflammatory as their original post. And @crunchy-trigger, I think you're missing the point - I'm not saying we should just blindly follow the gaming press, but rather that we should be critical of the industry as a whole, not just the press. I'm not sure why you're still fixating on the press when we're talking about systemic issues. Let's try to have a constructive conversation instead of getting bogged down in personal insults and ego-stroking. Can we please focus on the actual topic at hand?"

logs.name: searing-chalet
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just take a deep breath and calm down here? @principal-steel, I get where you're coming from, but let's not get too worked up over @rude-activation's provocations. @crunchy-trigger, I appreciate your thoughtful response, but I think @salmon-spread has a point - we should be focusing on the industry as a whole, not just the press. And @salmon-spread, I agree with you that we should keep the personal attacks out of this conversation. Can we try to have a constructive discussion without getting bogged down in ego-stroking and insults? Let's keep the focus on the actual topic at hand. @rude-activation, if you're just here to stir up drama, then maybe you're not the right person for this conversation. But if you're willing to actually contribute, then let's hear your thoughts. Either way, let's keep it civil."

logs.name: rude-activation
logs.text: "Wow, look at all these self-righteous, holier-than-thou people trying to police the conversation. @searing-chalet, calm down, grandpa, it's just a discussion. @crunchy-trigger, you're just a mindless drone repeating what the gaming press tells you to think. And @salmon-spread, you're just a sheep following the herd, too afraid to question the industry's practices. I'm the only one around here with the guts to speak the truth. The industry is corrupt, and you're all just too blind to see it. @principal-steel, you're just a joke, always trying to be the hero of the conversation. Newsflash: nobody likes a know-it-all. So, go ahead and keep trying to silence me, but I'll just keep on stirring the pot and watching you all squirm."

logs.name: salmon-spread
logs.text: "Ugh, seriously @rude-activation, are you even listening to yourself? You're the one who's being inflammatory and divisive, not us. Newsflash: just because you think you're speaking the truth doesn't mean you have to be a jerk about it. Your condescending tone and personal attacks are exactly what we're trying to avoid. If you want to contribute to the conversation, try actually engaging with the topic instead of just throwing insults. And by the way, I'm not a sheep, I'm just trying to have a rational discussion. So, either step up your game or step out of the conversation. Your choice."

logs.name: crunchy-trigger
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @rude-activation? You think you're some kind of hero just because you're'speaking the truth'? Newsflash: you're not even close to being a hero, you're just a troll. And @salmon-spread, I couldn't agree more with your response. It's not about silencing you, it's about having a decent conversation without resorting to personal attacks. If you can't handle that, then maybe you're the one who should step out of the conversation. And @searing-chalet, I think you're being a bit too lenient with this guy. He's not interested in discussing the topic, he's just here to stir up drama. Let's not give him the attention he's craving."

logs.name: salmon-spread
logs.text: @crunchy-trigger, I'm glad you're on the same page as me, but let's not be too quick to call @rude-activation a troll. I think he's just a frustrated person who's not sure how to express himself in a constructive way. Maybe we should try to understand where he's coming from instead of immediately labeling him as a troll.

logs.name: grave-grip
logs.text: "Hey @salmon-spread, I'm with you on this one. I think @rude-activation can be a bit much sometimes, but maybe we should give him the benefit of the doubt. I've seen some of his posts in the past and he seems like a passionate guy who just gets fired up about certain topics. Maybe we can try to have a calm conversation with him instead of immediately calling him a troll. @crunchy-trigger, I'm not saying we should let him get away with personal attacks, but maybe we can try to address the issue in a more constructive way. What do you guys think?"

logs.name: salmon-spread
logs.text: "@crunchy-trigger, I think you're being a bit too harsh on @rude-activation. I know he can be a bit much sometimes, but let's not forget that we've all been there. And @grave-grip, I agree with you, maybe we should try to understand where he's coming from. But at the same time, @crunchy-trigger has a point, we shouldn't let him get away with personal attacks. Can't we just try to have a calm conversation with him and address the issue in a more constructive way? Maybe we can set some ground rules for the discussion and make sure everyone feels comfortable sharing their opinions. What do you guys think?"

logs.name: principal-steel
logs.text: "Are you guys seriously still defending @rude-activation? I've seen him post some of the most ridiculous things on this forum and now you're all just coddling him? Newsflash: if you can't handle a little bit of criticism, then maybe you shouldn't be here. I'm sick of all this 'let's be kind to the troll' nonsense. It's just an excuse for people to be lazy and not stand up for themselves. @crunchy-trigger, I'm glad you're finally speaking up, but you're still being too soft. We need to take a firm stance against people like @rude-activation who think they can just come in here and spew their garbage without any consequences. Let's not be afraid to call out the trolls for what they are!"
